what: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
title: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
text: Constructs graph using 1D-array-based Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) format. structure highly optimized fast traversal algorithms like Dijkstra's. Separating graph construction path-finding algorithm avoids redundant processing running algorithms multiple times graph.

title: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
code: build_graph(n, u, v, w, directed = FALSE)

title: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
text: n integer specifying number nodes (vertices) graph. u integer vector starting nodes edge (1-indexed). v integer vector ending nodes edge (1-indexed). w numeric vector edge weights (costs). directed logical value indicating whether graph directed. Defaults FALSE (undirected).

title: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
text: build_graph() returns list containing internal graph representation (n, head, , weight, nxt, alloc_size).

title: Build a Graph in CSR-like Format — build_graph
Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
text: calibration_plot() generates calibration plot assess performance model's probabilistic predictions.

title: Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
code: calibration_plot(   actual,   predicted,   breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),   show_plot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
text: actual vector true outcomes. Must numeric vector containing 0 1. predicted numeric vector predicted probabilities, typically ranging 0 1. breaks numeric vector cut points used bin predicted values. Defaults seq(0, 1, = .1). show_plot logical. TRUE (default), plot displayed. FALSE, summary data returned without plotting. ... additional arguments passed plot() function. can used customize plot's title (main), color (col), point line types (type), etc.

title: Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
text: show_plot = TRUE, function draws plot side effect invisibly returns data frame summary statistics. show_plot = FALSE, visibly returns data frame. returned data frame includes following columns: bin bin number predictions assigned. n number observations bin. actual mean true outcomes bin (.e., fraction positives). predicted mean predicted probabilities bin.

title: Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
text: function groups predicted probabilities bins findInterval(predicted, breaks, rightmost.closed = TRUE, left.open = FALSE, .inside = FALSE), plots mean predicted probability (x-axis) fraction positive actual outcomes (y-axis) bin. perfectly calibrated model points lying diagonal line \(y = x\), indicating predicted probability , example, 0.8 corresponds 80 percent proportion positive outcomes.

title: Create Calibration Plots — calibration_plot
code: # Generate sample data n_obs <- 500 actual <- sample(0:1, n_obs, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.7, 0.3))  # Generate slightly miscalibrated predictions based on actuals predicted <- ifelse(actual == 1,                     rbeta(n_obs, shape1 = 4, shape2 = 1.5),                     rbeta(n_obs, shape1 = 1, shape2 = 4)) predicted <- pmin(pmax(predicted, 0), 1)  # Basic plot calibration_plot(actual, predicted)   # Customize the plot calibration_plot(actual, predicted,                  main = "Calibration Plot",                  xlab = "Mean Predicted Probability",                  ylab = "Observed Fraction of Positives",                  col = "maroon",                  pch = 19,                  cex = 1.2)  abline(0, 1, col = "gray50", lty = 2L)   # Get the summary data without plotting cal_data <- calibration_plot(actual, predicted, show_plot = FALSE) print(cal_data) #> # A tibble: 10 × 4 #>      bin     n actual predicted #>    <int> <int>  <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1     1   116 0         0.0483 #>  2     2    82 0.0122    0.146  #>  3     3    59 0.0169    0.245  #>  4     4    40 0.075     0.352  #>  5     5    39 0.282     0.446  #>  6     6    28 0.75      0.545  #>  7     7    31 0.839     0.640  #>  8     8    34 0.912     0.754  #>  9     9    38 1         0.852  #> 10    10    33 1         0.944

what: Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
title: Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
text: Computes shortest paths single source node nodes using graph constructed build_graph.

title: Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
text: graph list representing graph, returned build_graph. start_node integer specifying source node. inf numeric value representing infinity. Defaults 1e15.

title: Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
text: dijkstra() returns numeric vector length graph$n containing shortest distances.

title: Dijkstra's Algorithm on a Pre-built Graph — dijkstra
code: n <- 4 u <- c(1, 1, 2, 3) v <- c(2, 3, 4, 4) w <- c(2, 5, 1, 3) graph <- build_graph(n, u, v, w)  cost_from_1 <- dijkstra(graph, start_node = 1) cost_from_4 <- dijkstra(graph, start_node = 4)

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
text: `excess_plot()` creates mean median excess plot side-effect returns data frame invisibly.

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
code: excess_plot(   x,   method = c("mean", "median"),   mode = c("exact", "interpolate"),   show_plot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
text: x numeric vector. method "mean" "median". mode "exact" "interpolate". show_plot logical. ... optional arguments passed plot() function, main, xlab, ylab, col, etc.

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
text: `excess_plot()` returns data frame containing data used plotting. show_plot TRUE, data frame returned invisibly.

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
text: `excess_plot()` generates mean median excess plot, key tool visually assessing tail behavior distribution. Mean Excess Plot (MEP) represents function \(e(u) = \mathrm{E}[\;\mathrm{X} - u\mid\mathrm{X} > u\;]\), .e., average exceedances given threshold u. plot helps identifying type tail behavior. upward trend suggests heavy-tailed distribution, flat trend indicates exponential distribution, downward trend points light-tailed distribution. Median Excess Plot similar MEP, uses median exceedances instead mean. makes plot robust outliers.

title: Create Excess Plots — excess_plot
code: # Create a mean excess plot excess_plot(x = rexp(100))   # Create a median excess plot without showing the plot excess_data <- excess_plot(x = rexp(100), method = "median", show_plot = FALSE)  # Customize the plot appearance excess_plot(x = rexp(100), col = "maroon", main = "Mean Excess Plot")

title: Construct a Union-Find Structure — unionfind
text: Creates mutable Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure using environment. implements path compression union size efficient group operations.

title: Construct a Union-Find Structure — unionfind
text: n integer specifying number elements Union-Find structure.

title: Construct a Union-Find Structure — unionfind
text: unionfind() returns environment containing following property methods: parent integer vector size n. positive value indicates index parent node. negative value indicates node root, absolute value represents size group. find(x) function returns root index group containing x. unite(x, y) function unites groups containing x y. Returns TRUE groups successfully united, FALSE already group.

title: Construct a Union-Find Structure — unionfind
code: uf <- unionfind(5)  # Unite 1 and 2, 2 and 3 uf$unite(1, 2) #> [1] TRUE uf$unite(2, 3) #> [1] TRUE  # Are 1 and 3 in the same group? uf$find(1) == uf$find(3) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  # Are 1 and 4 in the same group? uf$find(1) == uf$find(4) # FALSE #> [1] FALSE
